data.title: GE Vernova wobbles after flagging larger-than-expected wind segment losses
data.description:
GE Vernova (GEV) is little changed in choppy trading early Wednesday after reporting better than expected Q4 adjusted earnings and earnings but a wider than expected EBITDA loss in its wind power unit.

The company said its wind segment could take a ~$250M revenue hit this year due to installation delays at the Vineyard Wind project offshore Massachusetts, and the failure to install 11 turbines at the project could lead to a low double-digit decline in revenue and account for ~$400M in losses.

GE Vernova (GEV) shares have more than doubled over the past year as investors bet that the company and peers will benefit as artificial intelligence drives power demand, but its wind business has struggled as the Trump administration has repeatedly moved to block offshore wind development.

While the wind segment "continues to generate losses, and equipment bookings suggest ongoing losses, we believe it is notable that bookings have started to grow on non-U.S. business, which should give the company opportunities to improve margins," Oppenheimer analyst Colin Rusch said in a note.

The wind-related update overshadowed an otherwise strong finish to 2025, as GE Vernova (GEV) said demand remained strong in its power and electrification units, which continue to benefit from rising electricity consumption driven by data centers, AI, and broader electrification.

CEO Scott Strazik said on GE Vernova's (GEV) earnings conference call, according to Reuters, that the company had signed more than $2B of electrification orders directly tied to data centers in 2025 and expects significant backlog growth in the power and electrification businesses in 2026.

The company said Q4 orders rose 65% organically to $22.2B, with gas power equipment backlog and slot reservation agreements expanding to 83 GW from 62 GW previously.

data.title: Mizuho upgrades Applied Materials to Outperform as WFE spending accelerates
data.description:
Applied Materials (AMAT) was upgraded to Outperform from Neutral by Mizuho Securities as they expect the world's No. 2 supplier of wafer fabrication equipment to benefit from rising capital expenditures in the U.S., Taiwan and Japan.

Mizuho also increased its price target on the stock to $370 from $275. Applied Materials had inched up 0.7% during early market action on Wednesday.

The financial firm projects that WFE spending will increase by 13% year over year in 2026 and another 12% in 2027. Mizuho expects Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Intel (INTC) to fuel much of this growth. The financial firm forecasts 2026 WFE spending of $134B and 2027 to total $150B.

"China, we believe, is less of a headwind as ex-China WFE revs (70% of revs) are accelerating faster," said Mizuho analysts, led by Vijay Rakesh, in an investor note.

"With strong AI demand driving leading-edge tool spend at <2nm, we now see China's share of global WFE declining to 23-25% in 2026-27E and a smaller drag on AMAT (~30% of AMAT revs), though still a substantial business with legacy PCVD, Sputtering, and Conductor etch," Rakesh added.

Spending for dynamic random access memory, which accounts for 29% of Applied Materials' revenue, is also expected to grow over the next two years.

data.title: Warner Bros. ticks lower amid report Paramount Skydance unlikely to raise bid
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD) edged lower by 0.4% amid a report that Paramount Skydance (PSKY) is unlikely to raise its $30 a share cash offer for the entertainment giant.

Paramount (WBD) and its financial backers, including billionaire Larry Ellison, aren't prepared to go higher than $30 a share, according to a Variety report  on Wednesday,  which cited people familiar with the thinking of Paramount and its financial backers.

The latest update comes after Paramount Skydance (PSKY) last week extended the tender offer deadline for its $108.4 billion hostile takeover of Warner Bros. (WBD) to Feb. 20 as it battles with Netflix (NFLX). 

Shares of Netflix (NFLX) ticked higher by 0.4% and Paramount Skydance (PSKT) edged higher by 0.2%.

data.title: Top performing information technology stocks in the past month
data.description:
As the first month of 2026 comes to an end this week and earnings season accelerates, below is a list of the top 10 information technology stocks above $10B market cap, ranked by their one-month price performance percentage. 

The list is dominated by Applied Digital (APLD), which recorded an impressive 71.93% one-month performance. Micron Technology (MU) is the second-best performer with a 44.05% gain and holds the highest Quant Rating among the top five, earning a Strong Buy rating of 4.99. MKS (MKSI), Entegris, (ENTG), and Western Digital Corporation (WDC) complete the top five performers.

Many stocks on the list, including MKS (MKSI), Entegris, (ENTG), Lam Research (LRCX), and Applied Materials (AMAT), belong to the Semiconductor Materials & Equipment industry. Onto Innovation Inc. (ONTO) is another notable performer that received a strong assessment, earning a Buy Quant Rating of 4.47.

Here is the list:

  1. Applied Digital Corporation (APLD), 1 month performance percentage: 71.93%

  2. Micron Technology, Inc. (MU), 1 month performance percentage: 44.05%

  3. MKS Inc. (MKSI), 1 month performance percentage: 39.84%

Tech ETFs: (VGT), (XLK), (IYW), (FTEC), (IXN), and (RSPT)

data.title: Amphenol crashes despite strong Q4 results, guidance
data.description:
Amphenol (APH) shares fell more than 13% on Wednesday, even as the cable and sensor manufacturer reported stronger-than-expected fourth-quarter results and guidance. 

Shares have gained more than 140% over the past 12 months, amid a strong run for data center suppliers. Investors may have had elevated expectations going into the results. For example, J.P. Morgan raised its price target on Amphenol to $185 from $160 on Tuesday, one day ahead of the results.

Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating has a Hold on Amphenol, given the elevated valuation.

For the period ending Dec. 31, Amphenol said it earned an adjusted $0.97 per share as revenue surged 49.1% year-over-year to come in at $6.44B. Organic revenue grew 37% compared to the fourth quarter of 2024. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $0.94 per share on $6.23B in revenue.

“We are pleased to have closed 2025 with record fourth quarter and full-year sales and Adjusted Diluted EPS, both significantly exceeding the high end of our guidance,” said Amphenol President and CEO, R. Adam Norwitt, in a statement. “Sales in the fourth quarter and for the full year increased from prior year by 49% and 52%, respectively, driven by strong organic growth in virtually all of our end markets, including exceptional organic growth in the IT datacom market, as well as contributions from the Company’s acquisition program. For both the quarter and full year we once again realized excellent profitability with Adjusted Operating Margin reaching 27.5% and 26.2%, respectively. We are extremely proud of the Company’s outstanding performance during the quarter and for the full year 2025.”

Amphenol also issued guidance for the coming quarter that was better-than-expected. For the first-quarter of the new fiscal year, the Wallingford, Ct.-based business expects to earn between $0.91 and $0.93 per share on an adjusted basis, above the $0.89 analysts were expecting. Sales are forecast to be between $6.9B and $7B, above the $6.55B estimate.

BNP Paribas analyst Andrew Buscaglia said the implied organic growth forecast was below estimates, which may be the reason shares fell on Wednesday. 

“APH is trading lower ...  on what we believe are concerns of an implied deceleration in organic sales in Q1 and perhaps lower contribution than expected from AI data center sales where most investors are focused,” Buscaglia wrote in a note to clients. “We’ll look for more color on the call around this, however, AI data center sales conversion from backlog can be uneven and the negative reaction may prove premature, in our view. APH didn’t yet mention anything on orders, which could also influence the stock and expectations for growth beyond Q1. Overall, our sense is this could prove to be a buying opportunity, particularly as APH saw broad-based growth across nearly all end markets, implying a broader recovery is underway which is only additive to the AI thesis.”

The company will host a conference call at 1 p.m. EST to discuss the results.

(This story has been updated to include analyst commentary.)

data.title: Intel in focus as CFO buys stock, Nvidia may use it for some of its 2028 production
data.description:
Intel (INTC) was in focus on Wednesday after the company's CFO disclosed a stock purchase, and tech giant Nvidia (NVDA) may use its foundry operations for some of its 2028 production.

Nvidia (NVDA) may use Intel's foundry for some of their needs in 2028 for chip manufacturing, Digitimes reported. Nvidia may wind up working with Intel on its upcoming Feynman architecture platform and may aim for “low volume, low-tier, non-core” production, the news outlet added, citing supply chain sources. The Jensen Huang-led Nvidia would still use Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) for its larger semiconductor manufacturing needs.

The news outlet added that the Feynman GPU die will remain with TSMC, but portions of the Input/Output (I/O) die could go to Intel's 18A manufacturing process, depending upon yield improvements. Advanced packaging may use Intel's EMIB technology, and Intel could handle approximately 25% of the final packaging, while Taiwan Semiconductor would be responsible for the remaining 75%. 

Nvidia and Taiwan Semiconductor did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha. An Intel spokesperson declined to comment.

Separately, on Wednesday, Intel CFO David Zinsner disclosed that he purchased 5,882 shares at $42.50 per share. The stock purchase was made on Jan. 26. 

Intel shares jumped 9% on Wednesday, while Nvidia shares rose more than 1.7%. Taiwan Semiconductor shares rose fractionally.

(This story has been updated with Intel's comment.)

data.title: ASML's record orders beat estimates amid AI-related demand
data.description:
Chip equipment maker ASML (ASML) posted record orders that surpassed estimates driven by AI related demand.

Shares of ASML fell about 1% on Wednesday. 

Key Metrics

ASML — which makes extreme ultraviolet lithography, or EUV, and Deep Ultraviolet, or DUV, lithography system — saw fourth quarter net sales increase about 5% year-over-year to €9.72B.

"The fourth quarter was particularly strong: we reported record total net sales of €9.7 billion, including the revenue recognized for two High NA systems. Our gross margin for Q4 was in line with our guidance at 52.2%," said ASML's CEO Christophe Fouquet. "In the last months, many of our customers have shared a notably more positive assessment of the medium-term market situation, primarily based on more robust expectations of the sustainability of AI-related demand. This is reflected in a marked step-up in their medium-term capacity plans and in our record order intake."

The Netherlands-based ASML reported net bookings of about €13.2B in the fourth quarter, beating an average estimate of €6.85B by analysts, Bloomberg News reported. Net bookings for EUV machines were €7.4B.

"It was a record quarter in terms of revenue. It was a record quarter in terms of order intake. It was a record quarter in terms of free cash flow generation," said ASML's CFO Roger Dassen.

Fouquet noted that the market outlook has notably improved in the last few months.

"This is especially true when it comes to the build-up of the capacity for AI applications, being data centers or other infrastructure. Now, we start to see that this build-up is also translating into need for capacity at our advanced customers. This is true for Logic. This is true for DRAM. This starts to translate also into orders for our most advanced technology, especially EUV," Fouquet added.

ASML's customers include leading chip manufacturers, including Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM) and Intel (INTC). 

China Sales

China accounted for 36% of the total net system sales of about €7.58B in the fourth quarter of 2025, followed by South Korea at 22% and the U.S. at 17%. Taiwan accounted for 13% of shipments of total net system sales. China accounted for 42% of the total net system sales of about €5.552B in the third quarter of 2025, while Taiwan was second with a 30% share.

SA Analyst Commentary

"Overall, this was a solid print. The last few quarters were marked by cautious messaging from management, with no guaranteed growth in 2026. That said, I think we are finally looking at the start of a semiconductor capex cycle, and the call had real signs of that shift," said Seeking Alpha analyst Semiconductor Analyst.

"Management sounded confident that AI-driven demand will support sales across both logic and memory. Long-term guidance was not revised higher, but the order intake was very strong, and that is likely to be the near-term driver for ASML stock," the analyst added.

Outlook

Fouquet said, "we expect 2026 to be another growth year for ASML's business, largely driven by a significant increase in EUV sales and growth in our installed base business sales."

Fouquet noted that there is strong demand for High Bandwidth Memory, or HBM, chips and also for Double Data Rate, or DDR, memory. Fouquet added that this "probably will lead to a very tight supply, at least in 2026 and most probably beyond that."

For the first quarter of 2026, ASML expects total net sales to be between €8.2B and €8.9B with a gross margin between 51% and 53%.

For the full year 2026, the company expects total net sales to be between €34B and €39B, with a gross margin between 51% and 53%.

Fouquet maintained ASML's long term outlook, in line with what the company said in November 2024, noting that in 2030 ASML expects revenue between €44B and €60B and a gross margin between 56% and 60%.

Wall Street Analysts

Jefferies kept its Hold rating on ASML's stock.

"ASML has reported blow-out orders of €13.2bn in Q4-25, almost double our forecast and consensus estimates. The order strength seems to have come from advanced logic and DRAM, with China staying muted. Q4-25 sales were slightly ahead of expectations," said analysts led by Janardan Menon.

The analysts noted that EUV sales are guided to increase significantly, with DUV sales being flat due to a decline in China compensated by an increase in advanced logic and DRAM.

"A significant portion of the bookings seems to be for 2027 as well, providing visibility for next year. While the stock's valuation multiples are high even with the 2026 guidance, the order strength and positive industry newsflow is likely to provide further medium-term upside," said Menon and his team.

BofA reiterated its Buy rating on ASML's shares.

"ASML saw a record 4Q results, with bookings of €13bn in 4Q significantly exceeding consensus expectations of €7bn. Sentiment around High-NA appears to be improving,with the company highlighting strong progress with customers and Intel accepting thefirst high-volume manufacturing tool," said analysts led by Didier Scemama.

The analysts believe there is upside in ASML's 2026 estimates, driven by China. The analysts added that their China import tracker implies €8.5B of China revenue for ASML in 2025 versus reported €8.1B, indicating there could be additional revenue recognition in the first quarter.

"ASML appears conservative on China incase of additional export control restrictions, which is part of the reason for their largerange of revenue guide for 26E, the other reason being lack of clean room space atseveral customers (we think in Memory/China in particular). We believe this leaves upside to ‘27E revenue growth and we model +21.7% growth next year (up from 16%prior)," said Scemama and his team.

Wells Fargo kept its Overweight rating on ASML's stock with a price target of $1,450.

"We view ASML's print positively given 1) blowout bookings qtr well ahead of expectations; 2) strong 2026 rev guide w/ a growth midpoint at +12% y/y (vs Street's +8% y/y & our view of buyside +10%-15%)," said analysts led by Joe Quatrochi.

Dividend and Buyback

ASML intends to declare a total dividend for the year 2025 of €7.50 per ordinary share, which is a 17% increase compared to 2024. An interim dividend of €1.60 per ordinary share will be made payable on Feb. 18. The company will make a final dividend proposal to the Annual General Meeting of €2.70 per ordinary share.

ASML announced a new share buyback program, effective Jan. 28 and to be executed by Dec. 31, 2028. The company intends to buy back shares worth up to €12B. The company expects a total of up to 2M shares will be used to cover employee share plans. ASML intends to cancel the remainder of the shares repurchased.

In the fourth quarter, ASML bought around €1.7B worth of shares under the 2022–2025 share buyback program. This program finished in December 2025, having repurchased a total of €7.6B out of the up to €12B program.

Shares of other chip equipment makers rose on Wednesday. Applied Materials (AMAT), Lam Research (LRCX) and KLA (KLAC) each rose nearly 1%.

Update: The story was updated with Wall Street analysts comments and stock movement.

data.title: Monro expects continued positive comp store sales in fiscal 2026 while advancing digital marketing and operational improvements
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Monro, Inc. (MNRO) Q3 2026

Management View

  • CEO Peter Fitzsimmons outlined the company's ongoing focus on four key areas: "driving profitable customer acquisition and activation, improving our store-based customer experience and selling effectiveness, increasing merchandising productivity... and real estate dispositions related to the previous closure of 145 underperforming stores." Fitzsimmons highlighted an expansion of the multichannel digital media plan to over 340 additional stores and noted the use of a new measurement framework for marketing impact, as well as the addition of call center support to 114 more stores, now covering over 830 locations.
  • Fitzsimmons reported that the company continued to expand usage of the ConfiDrive inspection tool and implemented analytical tools such as the District Manager Toolkit and a labor force optimization capability. The company also invested in field compliance support specialists to reduce administrative workloads for field leaders.
  • In merchandising, Fitzsimmons stated, "we focused heavily on ensuring inventory availability to present a well-developed product assortment to our guests during the fall and early winter selling season" and noted ongoing efforts to refine tire assortment and manage tariff impacts. He confirmed that tariffs "have not been as significant on either our customer pricing or our product cost as we anticipated."
  • Fitzsimmons disclosed that during the quarter, Monro exited 32 leases and sold 20 owned locations, resulting in proceeds of $17.3 million, with cumulative fiscal year-to-date proceeds from real estate disposition totaling $22.8 million. He indicated that the process is expected to generate positive cash flow and be largely completed in the coming quarters.
  • CEO Fitzsimmons noted, "the months of November and December as well as the third quarter marked the first time we delivered positive comps on a 2-year stack in over 2 years," and that this quarter was the fourth consecutive period of positive comps.
  • Brian D'Ambrosia, Executive VP of Finance, CFO & Treasurer, stated, "Sales decreased 4% to $293.4 million in the third quarter. This was primarily driven by a reduction in sales from the closure of 145 underperforming stores... partially offset by a 1.2% increase in comparable store sales from continuing store locations." He highlighted a 60 basis point year-over-year gross margin increase to 34.9% and detailed reductions in operating expenses, net interest expense, and inventory levels.

Outlook

  • D'Ambrosia reiterated, "We continue to expect to deliver year-over-year comparable store sales growth in fiscal 2026, primarily driven by our improvement plan as well as tariff-related price adjustments to our customers."
  • The company reaffirmed that the results of the store optimization plan will reduce total sales by approximately $45 million in fiscal 2026 and expects full-year gross margin to be consistent with fiscal 2025.
  • Reinforcement of capital allocation priorities was noted, with capital expenditures projected at $25 million to $35 million.
  • The company expects to generate sufficient cash flow to fund all capital allocation priorities, including dividends.

Financial Results

  • Sales for the quarter were $293.4 million.
  • Comparable store sales from continuing locations increased 1.2%.
  • The tire category was up 5%, though tire units declined 1%.
  • Gross margin expanded 60 basis points year-over-year to 34.9%.
  • Total operating expenses were $83.8 million, down from $94.8 million in the prior year period.
  • Operating income reached $18.6 million, with adjusted operating income at $10.3 million.
  • Net income was $11.1 million and diluted earnings per share was $0.35; adjusted diluted EPS was $0.16.
  • Inventory levels were reduced by over $7 million in the quarter, totaling more than $28 million in reductions since March.

Q&A

  • Tom Wendler, Stephens Inc.: Asked about the impact of digital marketing on same-store sales and the rollout to remaining stores. Fitzsimmons responded that stores receiving increased marketing support have outperformed others and that the rollout is based on operational readiness, with investments tailored by store needs.
  • David Lantz, Wells Fargo: Sought more detail on Q3 gross margin puts and takes and expectations for Q4. D'Ambrosia said, "we were 60 basis points better than the prior year... benefited by lower material costs... and 30 basis points of benefit from our occupancy costs," offset by higher technician labor costs. For Q4, the company expects gross margins above the prior year to achieve full-year consistency.
  • Lantz also asked about the potential benefits from Winter Storm Fern. Fitzsimmons indicated stores met demand and expect incremental sales from vehicle safety needs post-storm.
  • Bret Jordan, Jefferies: Inquired about comp ticket versus traffic contribution. D'Ambrosia noted traffic was down mid-single digits, offset by an average ticket increase.
  • Jordan also asked about regional performance and real estate values for remaining store dispositions. D'Ambrosia said strength was seen in the Northeast, with some weakness in the West, and that remaining owned stores held for sale are recorded on the balance sheet at approximately $5 million.
  • Brian Nagel, Oppenheimer: Questioned long-term comp store sales and expense leverage. Fitzsimmons explained that improvements should deliver incremental profit as comp sales and gross margin rise, but wage pressure and potential investments remain factors.
  • Nagel asked about the duration of benefits from storms and tax refunds. Fitzsimmons suggested both would positively impact short-term growth and that the spring selling season would benefit from ongoing initiatives.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts generally expressed a positive or slightly positive tone, congratulating management on the quarter and probing details on marketing, gross margin, and regional trends.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in prepared remarks and Q&A, with Fitzsimmons stating, "we are pleased with the progress we've made" and D'Ambrosia providing detailed explanations of financial performance and improvement plans.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts appear more constructive, with increased focus on the impact of digital and CRM marketing efforts and successful execution of the store optimization plan.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Both quarters emphasized the four key improvement areas, but the current quarter provided updates on further digital marketing rollout, more extensive call center coverage, and continued inventory reductions.
  • The current quarter highlighted the completion of significant real estate dispositions, while the previous quarter was still in the earlier stages of this process.
  • Gross margin improvement continued, up 60 basis points in Q3 versus 40 basis points in Q2, and inventory reductions accelerated.
  • Management's confidence increased, with Fitzsimmons noting, "this quarter marked the fourth consecutive period of positive comps" and providing more detailed outlook commentary.
  • Analysts' focus shifted from broader strategy and consumer pressure in Q2 to more execution- and impact-oriented questions in Q3.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management noted ongoing wage inflation and higher technician labor costs as a headwind.
  • Tariffs continue to be monitored, though their impact was less significant than initially anticipated.
  • Regional performance disparities were acknowledged, with some weakness in the West.
  • The company continues to manage the operational impact of store closures and real estate dispositions but expects these to be largely completed soon.
  • Analysts raised questions about the sustainability of comp sales gains, macroeconomic impacts (weather, tax refunds), and regional variation in business trends.

Final Takeaway

Monro’s management emphasized the company’s continued momentum in digital marketing, operational improvements, and real estate optimization, while highlighting sustained positive comparable store sales. The company reiterated its expectations for year-over-year comp store sales growth and stable gross margin for fiscal 2026, underpinned by disciplined marketing investment, ongoing cost controls, and a strong financial position. Management remains confident in Monro’s ability to deliver incremental profit and shareholder value as it completes key initiatives and capitalizes on positive industry trends.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Glaukos rises as FDA greenlights labeling update for iDose TR
data.description:
Glaukos Corporation (GKOS) added ~7% on Wednesday after the U.S. Food and Drug Administration approved a labeling change for the company’s eye implant iDose TR, paving the way for its repeat administration in patients with intraocular pressure.

The company stated that approval of its NDA labeling supplement application allows a repeat treatment protocol for iDose TR, which is indicated in the U.S. to treat intraocular pressure in patients with open-angle glaucoma or ocular hypertension.

“We are pleased to announce this important labeling enhancement for iDose TR, which should help expand access for patients who may benefit from repeat treatment and provide physicians with greater flexibility in managing their glaucoma patients over time,” said CEO Thomas Burns.

Glaukos (GKOS) noted that the approval validates the safety and tolerability of repeat use of the product as indicated in its iDose TR exchange trial, in which a second iDose TR implant demonstrated a favorable safety profile over 12 months.
